title: School-based support for emotion-related attendance challenges: effectiveness of @School when implemented with neurodiverse adolescents, their parents, and school staff
note: A Dutch study testing a school-based support program for neurodiverse teens who struggle to attend. One of the more practically grounded intervention papers I've come across — useful for thinking about what 'support' actually looks like in a school setting.

title: A coach-assisted, online parenting programme to support parents of adolescents who refuse school: evidence of acceptability and feasibility
note: An Australian online parenting program for families of adolescents who refuse school. Interesting because parents found it helpful and acceptable — but school attendance didn't actually improve. Raises real questions about whether parent-only approaches are sufficient.

title: Intervention for School Anxiety and Absenteeism in Children (ISAAC): Mixed-Method Feasibility Study of a Coach-Assisted, Parent-Focused Online Program
note: A UK feasibility study of a coach-supported online program for school anxiety and absenteeism. Early-stage work, but a good example of how digital delivery is being tested in this space.

title: School Refusal in Adolescence: Personality Traits and Their Influence on Treatment Outcome
note: Looks at personality traits in adolescents with school refusal and whether they predict how well treatment works. A reminder that 'school refusal' is not one thing — who the young person is matters enormously for outcomes.

title: Teachers’ experiences with the Back2School intervention—a pilot study addressing problematic school absenteeism
note: Teachers' experiences of a Norwegian school absenteeism intervention. What struck me here was how much coordination burden falls on school staff — a factor that rarely makes it into intervention design.

title: A systematic review of intervention programs for school attendance problems
note: A recent systematic review of intervention programs for school attendance problems more broadly. Good for situating the EBSA-specific literature within the wider attendance field.

title: Practitioner Review: School refusal: developments in conceptualisation and treatment since 2000
note: A foundational practitioner review of school refusal — covers how thinking in this area has shifted since 2000. Still one of the clearest accounts of why definitions matter so much for how we design and evaluate interventions.

title: Mapping the links between sexual addiction and gambling disorder: A Bayesian network approach
note: From colleagues at RMIT — uses network analysis to map the relationships between sexual addiction and gambling disorder. A different area from my own work, but a nice example of how complex behavioural patterns can be modelled without assuming simple cause and effect.

title: Large language models are homogeneously creative
note: Empirical work on AI systems and creative performance. Focuses on computational approaches to modelling creativity rather than clinical or educational application.

title: A new method for training creativity: narrative as an alternative to divergent thinking
note: Conceptual synthesis on AI and creativity. Emphasises theoretical mechanisms and broader implications rather than applied intervention work.

title: “The Cat Sat on the …?” Why Generative AI Has Limited Creativity
note: Applied cognitive research on AI-assisted creativity. Relevant for understanding decision processes under human–machine collaboration.

title: Brief Report: School Anxiety, School Attendance and School Refusal/Distress Following an Autism-Specific Parent-Mediated Intervention for Anxiety in Preschoolers
note: Autism-focused early intervention study examining downstream effects on school anxiety and attendance-related outcomes. Preventive rather than adolescent EBSA treatment work.

title: School refusal and truancy
note: Early clinical discussion of school refusal and truancy. Historically important but conceptually dated relative to modern EBSA frameworks.

title: Co-designing a virtual reality exposure therapy with students experiencing school anxiety: a proof-of-concept study
note: Proof-of-concept virtual reality exposure intervention for school anxiety. Experimental stage with limited generalisability.

title: The Link: An Alternative Educational Program in the Netherlands to Reengage School-Refusing Adolescents With Schooling
note: Structured re-engagement program (“The Link”) for school-refusing adolescents. Focus on reintegration rather than symptom reduction alone.

title: Synthesis without meta-analysis (SWiM) in systematic reviews: reporting guideline
note: SWiM guideline for narrative synthesis in systematic reviews. Methodological standard for non-meta-analytic evidence synthesis.

title: Modifiable Parent Factors Associated with Child and Adolescent School Refusal: A Systematic Review
note: Review of modifiable parental variables associated with school refusal. Highlights family-level intervention targets.

title: Dialectical Behavior Therapy for School Refusal: Treatment Development and Incorporation of Web-Based Coaching
note: Early adaptation of DBT principles for school refusal with digital coaching elements. Prototype-stage intervention development.

title: Individual characteristics of students with autism spectrum disorders and school refusal behavior
note: Examines autism-related traits associated with school refusal patterns. Supports heterogeneity in mechanisms underpinning attendance problems.

title: School refusal behavior in children and adolescents: a five-year narrative review of clinical significance and psychopathological profiles
note: Narrative synthesis of clinical presentations linked to school refusal. Emphasises comorbidity and heterogeneity over single-cause models.

title: Reengagement With Education: A Multidisciplinary Home-School-Clinic Approach Developed in Australia for School-Refusing Youth
note: Home–school–clinic re-engagement model integrating education and mental health services. Multi-system coordination emphasis.

title: Emotionally Based School Avoidance (EBSA): Students’ Views of What Works in a Specialist Setting
note: Student accounts of EBSA in specialist settings. Focus on perceived helpful conditions for re-engagement.

title: How to Understand School Refusal
note: Conceptual clarification of school refusal definitions. Focus on boundaries between related attendance constructs.

title: Developments in Classification, Identification, and Intervention for School Refusal and Other Attendance Problems: Introduction to the Special Series
note: Framing article introducing classification and intervention distinctions in attendance problems literature.

title: Differentiation Between School Attendance Problems: Why and How?
note: Clarifies distinctions between types of school attendance problems. Foundational for current EBSA classification debates.

title: A Scoping Review of Constructs Measured Following Intervention for School Refusal: Are We Measuring Up?
note: Reviews constructs used in post-intervention EBSA research. Identifies inconsistency in outcome measurement frameworks.

title: How to Understand School Refusal
note: Conceptual clarification of school refusal definitions. Focuses on distinguishing constructs within school attendance problems and implications for intervention design.

title: Developments in Classification, Identification, and Intervention for School Refusal and Other Attendance Problems: Introduction to the Special Series
note: Introductory framing paper for a special series on school attendance problems. Emphasises classification issues and how they shape intervention logic.

title: Differentiation Between School Attendance Problems: Why and How?
note: Key paper differentiating categories of school attendance problems. Central to modern EBSA taxonomy debates.

title: A Scoping Review of Constructs Measured Following Intervention for School Refusal: Are We Measuring Up?
note: Examines constructs used to measure outcomes after EBSA interventions. Identifies lack of consistency in outcome selection and reporting.

title: Developmental Issues Associated with Adolescent School Refusal and                     Cognitive-Behavioral Therapy Manuals
note: Practitioner-focused review of adolescent school refusal with emphasis on CBT manuals and developmental considerations.

title: School-based support for emotion-related attendance challenges: effectiveness of @School when implemented with neurodiverse adolescents, their parents, and school staff
note: School-based intervention for neurodiverse adolescents with attendance difficulties. Highlights multi-stakeholder coordination across school, family, and student systems.

title: An exploratory cluster-randomized controlled trial on mindfulness yoga’s effectiveness in school-refusing children: reductions in SCAS-C physical injury fears and pulse rate
note: Cluster RCT of mindfulness-based yoga for school refusal. Reports physiological and anxiety-related outcomes rather than attendance change alone.

title: School Refusal Assessment Scale--Revised
note: Standardised assessment scale for school refusal patterns (SRAS-R). Widely used in classification and functional assessment approaches.

title: School absenteeism and school refusal behavior in youth: A contemporary review
note: Foundational clinical review of school refusal and absenteeism. Establishes early integrative models still referenced in current frameworks.

title: Integrating Systemic and Analytic Approaches to School Attendance Problems: Synergistic Frameworks for Research and Policy Directions
note: Proposes integrated systemic and analytic model for understanding school attendance problems. Moves away from single-cause explanations.

title: Chronic Absenteeism: A Brief Review of Causes, Course and Treatment
note: Brief review of chronic absenteeism causes and treatment approaches. Broader than EBSA but commonly used in related synthesis.

title: School Refusal in Children and Adolescents: A Review of the Past 10 Years
note: Early review of school refusal in child and adolescent psychiatry. Establishes clinical baseline for later intervention literature.

title: Intervention for School Anxiety and Absenteeism in Children (ISAAC): Mixed-Method Feasibility Study of a Coach-Assisted, Parent-Focused Online Program
note: Feasibility study of coach-assisted online intervention for school anxiety and absenteeism. Early-stage evidence on digital delivery models.

title: Perfect storm: emotionally based school avoidance in the post-COVID-19 pandemic context
note: Online parenting intervention for emotionally based school avoidance. High acceptability but limited behavioural impact on attendance outcomes.

title: Rapid Return for School Refusal: A School-Based Approach Applied With Japanese Adolescents
note: Rapid return school-based CBT intervention in adolescent sample. Focus on structured reintegration into school attendance.

title: Treatment for School Refusal Among Children and Adolescents
note: Systematic review and meta-analysis of interventions for school refusal. Suggests overall positive effects but highlights weak methodological consistency across studies.

title: Intervention for school anxiety and absenteeism in children (ISAAC): Co-designing a brief parent-focused intervention for emotionally-based school avoidance
note: Co-designed parent-focused intervention for emotionally based school avoidance. Emphasises acceptability and feasibility over efficacy at this stage.

title: Reengagement With Education: A Multidisciplinary Home-School-Clinic Approach Developed in Australia for School-Refusing Youth
note: Multi-disciplinary home–school–clinic model for school-refusing youth. Focuses on coordinated service delivery across education and clinical systems.

title: Augmenting Cognitive Behavior Therapy for School Refusal with Fluoxetine: A Randomized Controlled Trial
note: RCT combining CBT with fluoxetine for school refusal. Suggests potential additive benefit but raises questions about pharmacological contribution.

title: The Kids and Teens at School (KiTeS) Framework: An Inclusive Bioecological Systems Approach to Understanding School Absenteeism and School Attendance Problems
note: KiTeS bioecological framework for understanding school attendance problems. Positions EBSA within multi-layered environmental systems.

title: Individual characteristics of students with autism spectrum disorders and school refusal behavior
note: Examines ASD-related characteristics associated with school refusal. Reinforces heterogeneity in underlying mechanisms.

title: Fostering school reintegration after psychiatric inpatient treatment: description and study protocol of an evaluation study about a rehabilitation program for children and adolescents with chronic school refusal (SchuTIng-stAR)
note: Protocol for post-psychiatric inpatient reintegration program. Focus on structured return-to-school pathways after severe mental health episodes.

title: School refusal and bullying in children with autism spectrum disorder
note: Links bullying experiences with school refusal in autistic children. Suggests social environment as a key mediating factor.

title: Autistic girls and emotionally based school avoidance: supportive factors for successful re-engagement in mainstream high school
note: Explores supportive factors for autistic girls re-engaging in mainstream schooling. Emphasises contextual and relational supports.

title: How can the education sector support children’s mental health? Views of Australian healthcare clinicians
note: Clinician perspectives on how education systems can support child mental health. Focuses on system-level rather than individual interventions.

title: A systematic review of intervention programs for school attendance problems
note: Broad review of interventions for school attendance problems. Useful for situating EBSA-specific interventions within wider attendance literature.

title: The Treatment of School Avoidance in Children and Adolescents With Psychiatric Illness
note: Randomised controlled trial of treatment approaches for school avoidance in clinical populations. Early evidence base with limited generalisability.

title: Multifamily Therapy for Adolescents With School Refusal: Perspectives of the Adolescents and Their Parents
note: Qualitative evidence on multifamily therapy for school refusal. Highlights perceived benefits in family communication and support.

title: SPIRAL: parents’ experiences of emotionally-based school non-attendance (EBSNA) informing a framework for successful reintegration
note: Parent experiences of emotionally based school non-attendance. Develops framework for reintegration based on lived experience.

title: Effectiveness of cognitive-behavioral group play therapy on anxiety-based school refusal and behavioral problems in elementary school boys: a preliminary randomized controlled trial
note: Preliminary RCT of CBT-based group play therapy for anxiety-related school refusal. Early evidence, limited sample and scope.

title: A coach-assisted, online parenting programme to support parents of adolescents who refuse school: evidence of acceptability and feasibility
note: Online parenting program for adolescent school refusal. High acceptability but limited measurable improvement in attendance outcomes.

title: Does multifamily therapy help parents of adolescents with anxiety-based school refusal? A qualitative approach
note: Qualitative study of multifamily therapy effects on parents. Emphasises relational and systemic change processes.

title: ROBINS-I: a tool for assessing risk of bias in non-randomised studies of interventions
note: ROBINS-I tool for assessing bias in non-randomised intervention studies. Methodological standard rather than substantive EBSA content.

title: RoB 2: a revised tool for assessing risk of bias in randomised trials
note: RoB 2 tool for assessing bias in randomised trials. Key methodological reference for intervention evaluation.

title: Adverse Childhood Experiences and Education Outcomes among Adolescents: Linking Survey and Administrative Data
note: Links adverse childhood experiences to education outcomes. Indirect relevance to EBSA via risk factor pathways.

title: Outcome of a Multi-modal CBT-based Treatment Program for Chronic School Refusal
note: Multi-modal CBT-based treatment for chronic school refusal. Reports outcomes for structured clinical intervention.

title: School refusal and anxiety among children and adolescents: A systematic scoping review
note: Scoping review of school refusal and anxiety. Synthesises broad literature without strict intervention focus.

title: A grounded theory study of family coach intervention with persistent school non-attenders
note: Grounded theory study of family coach interventions for persistent non-attendance. Focus on process rather than outcomes.

title: A systematic review of school refusal
note: Systematic review of school refusal literature. Consolidates definitions, causes, and intervention approaches.

title: Transition matters: pupils’ experiences of the primary–secondary school transition in the West of Scotland and consequences for well‐being and attainment
note: Examines school transition effects on wellbeing and attendance. Highlights developmental turning points in attendance trajectories.

title: Impact of COVID-2019 on school attendance problems
An exploration of approaches to supporting young people experiencing emotionally based school avoidance (EBSA) in a social, emotional and mental health (SEMH) school
Editorial: Addressing emotionally based school avoidance: causes, consequences, and interventions
